title: Gomesa ciliata
taxonomy: Gomesa ciliata is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This epiphytic orchid is also known as the 'Velvet Pouch Orchid' due to its unique appearance. Gomesa ciliata belongs to the subtribe Oncidiinae, which is a subcategory of the Oncidiinae tribe. It is native to South America and is often found in humid environments. Within the subtribe Oncidiinae, Gomesa ciliata is placed in the genus Gomesa.

It is often used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and sore throats. Gomesa ciliata has also been studied for its potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. However, more research is required to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Gomesa ciliata.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various ailments such as wounds, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
